item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least 11 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: 5/13: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: President Trump's trip to China gets underway; DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin details what ICE agents will be doing at World Cup games.

item.title: Musk flew to China near end of OpenAI trial, after judge warned he could be recalled
item.description: Elon Musk arrived in Beijing on Wednesday, as his $150 billion lawsuit against OpenAI's Sam Altman played out. But a judge told Musk last month he may be recalled to a California courtroom for further testimony at the request of OpenAI lawyers.

item.title: Denise Powell projected to win Democratic primary in key Nebraska district
item.description: The state's electoral system was a key issue in the 2nd Congressional District primary to replace GOP Rep. Don Bacon.

item.title: What led to the so-called "learning recession" in American schools?
item.description: The annual Education Scorecard is highlighting a long-term decline in learning among American students. CBS News national correspondent Meg Oliver breaks down the results.

item.title: How Taiwan factors into Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping
item.description: President Trump is in Beijing for a high-stakes meeting with his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping. He last visited Beijing nearly a decade ago in 2017. This time around, the two are expected to discuss trade, the Middle East and Taiwan. Jonathan Czin, a former senior China analyst at the CIA, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Why Taiwan is a major flashpoint between the U.S. and China
item.description: For decades, U.S. presidents have remained steadfast in their defense of the tiny Asian ally from its neighboring giant.

item.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions, orders new trial
item.description: Alex Murdaugh was convicted of his killing his wife and his son at the family's home in 2021.

item.title: Trump arrives in Beijing to meet with Xi Jinping
item.description: President Trump said before he left that he and President Xi Jinping "have a lot of things to discuss."

item.title: Body of second U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco recovered
item.description: The Army identified the soldier as Spc. Mariyah Collington.

item.title: Extended interview: DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin on ICE at the World Cup, hantavirus and more
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin speaks with CBS News' Nicole Sganga in this exclusive interview about a number of topics, including ICE's role at the World Cup, "Alligator Alcatraz," the U.S. response to hantavirus and more.

item.title: Is the rise of a powerful new China a true narrative?
item.description: As President Trump and China's Xi Jinping prepare to meet, Americans will hear a lot about American decline and the rise of a powerful new China. The Chinese certainly believe it - but is it true? Tony Dokoupil has more.

item.title: New video shows dramatic Air Force rescue after plane crash off Florida coast
item.description: The U.S. military rescued a small plane that crashed off Florida's East Coast on Tuesday. The passengers were stranded at sea for hours. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: DHS chief Mullin says agency has no plan to shut down "Alligator Alcatraz"
item.description: DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin said the department has no plan to shut down Alligator Alcatraz, following reports that companies hired by Florida to operate the detention center were told it would close.

item.title: American monitored for hantavirus: "I'm just ready to cope" with quarantine
item.description: Jake Rosmarin is one of the 16 Americans at the University of Nebraska Medical Center being monitored for signs of hantavirus. Ian Lee spoke with him and has more details.

item.title: Alex Murdaugh's lawyers say they "look forward to a new trial"
item.description: Alex Murdaugh's double murder trial drew national attention, but three years later, South Carolina's Supreme Court tossed aside his conviction. Mark Strassmann reports on the new developments.

item.title: Inflation is getting worse, Americans are feeling the impact
item.description: Wholesale prices in the U.S. jumped 6% last month compared to a year ago, the highest annual increase in more than three years. Gas prices are driving the increase, and Americans are feeling the impact. Jo Ling Kent reports.

item.title: China's most critical issue is Taiwan, also central to America's economy
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping has called Taiwan "the most important issue" between his country and the U.S. About 10% of the population in Taiwan wants to reunify with mainland China, and there are real fears that it will fall under communist control. Tony Dokoupil and Anna Coren break down its importance to China and the U.S.

item.title: Utah woman's hopes of flipping mansion flop after murder charge
item.description: Utah woman accused of murdering husband loses $12 million real estate deal after his death.

item.title: Trump given red carpet welcome in Beijing
item.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping will meet face-to-face for the first time on this trip at an elaborate welcome ceremony. The two-day visit is meant to signal stability to the world, showing that the competition between the U.S. and China is not escalating into confrontation. Weijia Jiang has more from Beijing.

item.title: Did Utah mom poison her husband, then write a children's book on grief?
item.description: When her husband Eric died in March of 2022, Kouri Richins wrote a children's book to help her sons cope with the loss of their father – then she was charged in his death. Follow the timeline for a deep dive into the history of Eric and Kouri's relationship.

item.title: Why Alex Murdaugh's murder conviction was overturned
item.description: Three years after Alex Murdaugh was convicted of killing his wife and son, the South Carolina Supreme Court on Wednesday overturned the verdict and granted Murdaugh a new trial. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman reports.

item.title: DHS Secretary Mullin says ICE won't "round up" people at World Cup
item.description: Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin told CBS News that ICE arrests at the FIFA World Cup are not off the table, but the agency will not be at the global sporting event for the purpose of immigration arrests.

item.title: How improved U.S.-China relationship could benefit the world
item.description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday morning ahead of a high-stakes meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Anna Coren and Natalie Brand report.

item.title: Markwayne Mullin details ICE's role at World Cup games
item.description: It's been less than two months since Markwayne Mullin was sworn in as the Secretary of Homeland Security. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga sat down with him for an exclusive interview.

item.title: 5/13: CBS Evening News
item.description: President Trump in China amid economic pressure at home caused by Iran war; Alex Murdaugh to be tried again after court overturns murder convictions.

item.title: AI is fabricating citations in biomedical studies, researchers find
item.description: "Your doctor could be making decisions around treatment based on studies that never existed," one expert said.

item.title: CEOs worth nearly $1 trillion accompany Trump to China
item.description: The delegation of business leaders underscores the deep ties many major U.S. companies maintain with China despite years of trade tensions.

item.title: U.S. students' "reading recession" continuing but some places bucking the trend
item.description: Researchers say the U.S. is experiencing a "reading recession" that predates the pandemic. But some places are bucking the trend, chalking up higher test scores.

item.title: Pennsylvania woman finds 3.09-carat white diamond at Arkansas state park
item.description: A woman from Pennsylvania found a 3.09-carat white diamond at Crater of Diamonds State Park in Arkansas.
